topic: A college student discovers their roommate has been reading their journal
raw_output:
[story 1]

The air in the dorm room felt thick, suffocating. Every time I glanced over at Liam, a cold knot tightened in my stomach. He was engrossed in a thick, leather-bound book, the lamplight casting deep shadows across his face. I’d been unusually quiet lately, picking at the frayed edges of my textbook, trying to maintain a façade of normalcy while my nerves frayed at the edges. Then, I saw it—a page in my journal, tucked between pages of his novel. My breath hitched. My hands started to tremble, the cheap plastic of my pen digging into my palm. I slowly turned my head, hoping he hadn't noticed the sudden stillness radiating off me. He didn't look up, just turned a page with a soft, almost inaudible rustle. A slow, creeping dread began to coil in my chest, making it hard to draw a full breath.

[story 2]

I kept my movements measured, deliberately slow, as I navigated the cramped space between our desks. The silence between us was loud, filled only by the frantic drumming in my own ears. Liam was sitting at his desk, seemingly absorbed in his studies, but I could feel his awareness, a subtle shift in his posture that made the hairs on my arms prickle. My gaze drifted involuntarily to the open journal lying face-up on my nightstand. It was a mistake, a lapse in concentration, but the sight of my private thoughts laid bare felt like a physical blow. A sharp intake of air escaped my lungs. I forced myself to look away, focusing instead on the pattern of the worn carpet. When I dared to look back, Liam was staring directly at the page, his expression unreadable, a strange, unnerving stillness about him. The silence stretched, taut and brittle, threatening to shatter.

[story 3]

My fingers were slick against the cover of my notebook as I tried to focus on the calculus problems, but my attention kept snagging on the shared space. Liam was reading, his brow furrowed in concentration, and I felt an unwelcome scrutiny settle upon me. A deep, unsettling feeling washed over me, making my jaw ache. It was the feeling of being watched, of every secret I’d ever scribbled onto those pages suddenly exposed. I swallowed hard, the dryness in my throat making the action difficult. I risked a quick peek. The ink of my handwriting was visible, stark against the cream paper. My heart began to hammer against my ribs, a frantic rhythm that felt entirely too loud in the quiet room. When I finally pulled my eyes away, I noticed Liam’s gaze lingering for a beat too long before he returned to his book, a flicker of something unreadable crossing his features.
